fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer occupational therapist license to indiana, occupational therapist compact indiana, indiana occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Indiana PLA — Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, 402 W Washington St, Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Indiana.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense.IN.gov Account
steps.description: Visit MyLicense.IN.gov and click 'Register an Account' to establish your applicant profile, which will be used to submit and manage your application.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your MyLicense.IN.gov account, select 'Occupational Therapist' under your profession, and complete the specific application type (e.g., Original License by Examination or Original License by Endorsement).

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your academic institution to send official, sealed transcripts directly to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, confirming your qualifying degree.

steps.title: Send NBCOT Score Report
steps.description: Have the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) send your official score report for the OTR examination directly to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee through the MyLicense.IN.gov portal using a credit card to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Indiana offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Indiana PLA — Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Indiana is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Indiana without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Indiana also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $50. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Indiana has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Indiana is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Indiana without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Indiana also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Indiana
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Indiana, you must submit your application online through the MyLicense.IN.gov portal, managed by the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to MyLicense.IN.gov, click 'Register an Account' at the top right, and follow the prompts to create your personal account using a valid email address and setting up your security questions.
